source.section: AFCS (Automatic Flight Control System) > CPL (Coupled Steering) Mode > CPL WYPT Sub-Mode Example

steps.action: Make sure your stick is centered, no force is applied on it and that you have an appropriate deadzone (see CONTROLS SETUP - WHAT YOU NEED MAPPED section).
steps.voiced: Confirm stick is centered with no input force and deadzone is configured.

steps.action: Select a desired waypoint using the HSI format.
steps.voiced: Select the desired waypoint on the HSI format.

steps.action: Activate the WYPT navigation mode by pressing OSB next to WYPT. WYPT is boxed when selected.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to WYPT to activate WYPT navigation mode — confirm WYPT boxes.

steps.action: If desired, select AUTO to automatically follow the desired waypoint sequence (SEQ1 in our case).
steps.voiced: Optionally select AUTO to follow the desired waypoint sequence automatically.

steps.action: If desired, click and hold (Left/Right mouse button) the CRS (Course Set) knob to set desired course (CSEL) to intercept the waypoint from.
steps.voiced: Optionally set the CRS knob for desired course to intercept the waypoint.

steps.action: Press the AP button on the UFC to show the autopilot options.
steps.voiced: Press AP on the UFC to show autopilot options.

steps.action: Press the OSB adjacent “CPL” to activate coupled autopilot mode.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to CPL to activate coupled autopilot mode.

steps.action: A colon will appear (“:CPL”) to indicate that it is active. “CPL WYPT” will appear on both the HSI and HUD.
steps.voiced: Confirm :CPL is active and CPL WYPT appears on HSI and HUD.

steps.action: The autopilot will follow the selected waypoint (or waypoint sequence if AUTO is selected).
steps.voiced: Confirm autopilot follows the selected waypoint or sequence.

steps.action: You can disengage autopilot by pressing the Paddle Switch on the control stick.
steps.voiced: Press the Paddle Switch on the control stick to disengage autopilot.
